vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: strict-transport-security
vulnerabilities.description: The HTTP response is missing the strict-transport-security security header.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure your web server to send the strict-transport-security header.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: content-security-policy
vulnerabilities.description: The HTTP response is missing the content-security-policy security header.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure your web server to send the content-security-policy header.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: x-frame-options
vulnerabilities.description: The HTTP response is missing the x-frame-options security header.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure your web server to send the x-frame-options header.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: x-content-type-options
vulnerabilities.description: The HTTP response is missing the x-content-type-options security header.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure your web server to send the x-content-type-options header.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: referrer-policy
vulnerabilities.description: The HTTP response is missing the referrer-policy security header.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure your web server to send the referrer-policy header.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: permissions-policy
vulnerabilities.description: The HTTP response is missing the permissions-policy security header.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure your web server to send the permissions-policy header.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: x-xss-protection
vulnerabilities.description: The HTTP response is missing the x-xss-protection security header.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure your web server to send the x-xss-protection header.

vulnerabilities.name: Redis Lua Sandbox < 8.2.2 - Cross-User Escape
vulnerabilities.description: Redis is an open source, in-memory database that persists on disk. Versions 8.2.1 and below allow an authenticated user to use a specially crafted Lua script to manipulate different LUA objects and potentially run their own code in the context of another user. The problem exists in all versions of Redis with LUA scripting. This issue is fixed in version 8.2.2. A workaround to mitigate the problem without patching the redis-server executable is to prevent users from executing LUA scripts. This can be done using ACL to block a script by restricting both the EVAL and FUNCTION command families.

vulnerabilities.name: Redis < 8.2.1 lua script - Integer Overflow
vulnerabilities.description: Redis is an open source, in-memory database that persists on disk. Versions 8.2.1 and below allow an authenticated user to use a specially crafted Lua script to cause an integer overflow and potentially lead to remote code execution The problem exists in all versions of Redis with Lua scripting. This issue is fixed in version 8.2.2.

vulnerabilities.name: Redis  < 8.2.1 Lua Long-String Delimiter - Out-of-Bounds Read
vulnerabilities.description: Redis is an open source, in-memory database that persists on disk. Versions 8.2.1 and below allow an authenticated user to use a specially crafted LUA script to read out-of-bound data or crash the server and subsequent denial of service. The problem exists in all versions of Redis with Lua scripting. This issue is fixed in version 8.2.2. To workaround this issue without patching the redis-server executable is to prevent users from executing Lua scripts. This can be done using ACL to block a script by restricting both the EVAL and FUNCTION command families.

vulnerabilities.name: Redis Lua Parser < 8.2.2 - Use After Free
vulnerabilities.description: Redis is an open source, in-memory database that persists on disk. Versions 8.2.1 and below allow an authenticated user to use a specially crafted Lua script to manipulate the garbage collector, trigger a use-after-free and potentially lead to remote code execution. The problem exists in all versions of Redis with Lua scripting. This issue is fixed in version 8.2.2. To workaround this issue without patching the redis-server executable is to prevent users from executing Lua scripts. This can be done using ACL to restrict EVAL and EVALSHA commands.

vulnerabilities.name: Redis - Default Logins
vulnerabilities.description: Redis service was accessed with easily guessed credentials.

vulnerabilities.name: Redis Server - Unauthenticated Access
vulnerabilities.description: Redis server without any required authentication was discovered.

vulnerabilities.name: TLS Version - Detect
vulnerabilities.description:
TLS version detection is a security process used to determine the version of the Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocol used by a computer or server.
It is important to detect the TLS version in order to ensure secure communication between two computers or servers.

vulnerabilities.name: HTTP Missing Security Headers
vulnerabilities.description: This template searches for missing HTTP security headers. The impact of these missing headers can vary.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Subresource Integrity
vulnerabilities.description: Checks if external script and stylesheet tags in the HTML response are missing the Subresource Integrity (SRI) attribute.

vulnerabilities.name: Detect SSL Certificate Issuer
vulnerabilities.description: Extract the issuer's organization from the target's certificate. Issuers are entities which sign and distribute certificates.

vulnerabilities.name: SSL DNS Names
vulnerabilities.description: Extract the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) from the target's certificate. SAN facilitates the usage of additional hostnames with the same certificate.

vulnerabilities.name: Add DOM EventListener - Detection
vulnerabilities.description: Identifies the use of JavaScript addEventListener calls in the DOM.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing HSTS Header
vulnerabilities.description: The Strict-Transport-Security header is not set, allowing downgrade attacks.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing X-Frame-Options
vulnerabilities.description: Page can be embedded in iframes, enabling clickjacking attacks.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing X-Content-Type-Options
vulnerabilities.description: Browser may MIME-sniff responses, enabling content injection.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Content-Security-Policy
vulnerabilities.description: No CSP header detected, increasing XSS attack surface.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Referrer-Policy
vulnerabilities.description: Browser may leak URL data in Referer headers.
